title: Foolish Wives

evidence.note: Published 1922; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — Foolish Wives (1922), full surviving film

editorial: Foolish Wives is a 1922 American silent drama written and directed by Erich von Stroheim and produced by Universal Pictures, with von Stroheim also starring as a con-artist count on the Riviera. Promoted by Universal as the 'first million-dollar movie,' its lavish cost and frank subject matter made it notorious, and it survives today only in truncated form. Published in 1922, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
